_comment: Pinned snapshot of the ax-hub-cli failure contract (spec 004 FR-012). Source of truth: ax-hub-cli crates/axhub-core/src/{exit_code.rs,error.rs} + axhub/src/main.rs::error_to_error_code. The empathy catalog (data/catalog.json) MUST key every entry on a base exit code present in `exit_codes`. Drift is enforced by tests/exit-contract-parity.test.ts (SC-008). When the CLI contract changes, the parity test fails -> re-verify against the CLI and update this snapshot.
plugin_context_subcodes_note: validation.* / github.* / open.* / catalog.* / profile.* / apis.* are PLUGIN-side context refinements. The current CLI does NOT emit them as error.subcode (Usage/NotFound/etc. -> subcode None). Skills select these catalog entries by command context, not by CLI subcode. Their catalog key BASE must still resolve to a valid CLI exit code so classify()'s base fallback is coherent.

helper_output_exit_codes._comment: SECOND frozen contract (spec 004). The axhub-helpers binaries (preflight/deploy_prep/bootstrap/list_deployments/token-gate/quality_gate/sync) keep a sysexits-derived OUTPUT contract that predates the CLI's 4/5/6 migration and is a TESTED PUBLIC surface (e.g. EXIT_LIST_AUTH=65, preflight::EXIT_AUTH=65) that MUST NOT change. Skills that read helper exits (deploy via deploy-prep, apps via preflight, init via bootstrap, recover via list-deployments) see THESE numbers. The catalog is keyed on the CLI space; helper exits normalize into it (see helper_output_normalization). The catalog therefore does NOT carry 65/67/68/70 keys, and the parity test still requires catalog keys subset of `exit_codes`.

helper_output_normalization._comment: classify-exit (catalog.rs::normalize_helper_exit) folds the helper-output space into the CLI space before catalog lookup, so both frozen contracts route to one empathy template. Mirrors list_deployments::exit_to_error_code (4|65=>auth, 5|67=>not_found). 64/66 are shared and pass through unchanged.
